name: Stripe
one_liner: Payments platform whose REST API lets developers accept payments and manage customers, subscriptions, invoices, payouts, and more.
access_notes: Free Stripe account gives immediate test-mode (sandbox) secret/publishable API keys from the dashboard with no payment or approval; live-mode keys require activating the account but the full API is usable in test mode instantly.
api_notes: Large REST API: resource-oriented URLs, form-encoded requests, JSON responses. Covers hundreds of operations across Payments, Charges, Customers, PaymentIntents, Subscriptions/Billing, Invoices, Products, Payouts, Connect, Checkout, Terminal, and more. Official SDKs in many languages. OAuth is used for Connect (platform) flows.
mcp_notes: Stripe ships an official MCP server: a remote hosted server at https://mcp.stripe.com (OAuth) and a local server via `npx -y @stripe/mcp --tools=all --api-key=...`. Part of the Stripe Agent Toolkit, documented at docs.stripe.com/mcp.
main_blocker: None for building/testing: public REST API with self-serve test keys. Going live with real payments requires account activation/business verification, but development and integration need no approval.

evidence.claim: Stripe API is REST-based with API-key auth and full sandbox/test-mode support.
Stripe supports webhook endpoints (up to 16 per account) for event notifications.
Stripe hosts an official remote MCP server at mcp.stripe.com and a local @stripe/mcp server via npx.

open_questions: Exact live-mode activation requirements vary by country/business type, but these do not affect API buildability in test mode.
